bible.heroSubtitle: Discover how Africa and its peoples are intimately linked to sacred history, forming the origins of an unshakeable millennial faith.

racines.title: The Roots: Abraham and Egypt
racines.p1: The rich history of the African continent is deeply rooted in the stories of the patriarchs. Abraham found refuge in Egypt during the famine, marking the first strong link of interdependence between the original promise of Israel and the vast protective land of Africa.
racines.p2: Over the centuries, the nation of Israel developed within powerful ancient Egypt, inevitably absorbing its culture, architecture, and wisdom before beginning the great saving Exodus to the Promised Land.
racines.p3: Sinai and the burning desert, this mystical bridge that geographically and religiously connects Africa to the Middle East, became the majestic cradle of the Law, Revelation, and the eternal divine Covenant.
racines.quote1: The Lord said to Abram: 'Leave your country, your kindred and your father's house, and go to the land that I will show you.'
racines.quote2: These are the names of the sons of Israel who came to Egypt...

sagesse.title: Wisdom: The Queen of Sheba and Solomon
sagesse.p1: The sumptuous and legendary meeting between the prestigious King Solomon, endowed with divine wisdom, and the Queen of Sheba marks a diplomatic, economic, but also deeply spiritual alliance between radiant Jerusalem and the rich Horn of Africa.
sagesse.p2: Beyond the gold, precious stones, and spices that the Bible details in profusion, these exchanges fused and shaped a religious and scholarly imagery deeply shared by both peoples.
sagesse.p3: This politico-religious heritage remains extremely vibrant today, especially in ancestral Ethiopian traditions that faithfully link their powerful royal lineage (the Negus) to this majestic biblical meeting at the crossroads of two worlds.
sagesse.quote1: The Queen of Sheba heard of Solomon's fame because of the name of the Lord. She came to test him with hard questions. She arrived in Jerusalem with a very large retinue...

exil.title: Exile and Welcome: Jesus in Egypt
exil.p1: The Flight into Egypt is one of the founding and most symbolic moments of the dawn of the New Testament. Faced with the destructive madness and mortal jealousy of King Herod, it was benevolent Africa that instantly offered saving refuge to the Holy Family.
exil.p2: Africa has always been celebrated as the land of asylum par excellence in the Bible, the providential place where the innocent and cornered divine and human find comfort, resources, and ultimate protection to guarantee and preserve the lineage.
exil.p3: This spiritual symbolism of incredible power still resonates today: yesterday's land of exile is tomorrow's original foundation. The African continent is glorified for having physically protected Him who came in spirit to protect and save the entire world.
exil.quote1: When they had gone, an angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream. 'Get up,' he said, 'take the child and his mother and escape to Egypt. Stay there until I tell you...'

premices.title: The Firstfruits of Salvation: The Ethiopian Eunuch
premices.p1: On the dusty and deserted road descending from Jerusalem to Gaza, a founding encounter ordered by the Holy Spirit occurred between the apostle Philip and a magnificently dressed high dignitary: the eunuch, the powerful minister of the Candace, Queen of Ethiopia.
premices.p2: Ardently and humbly seeking to understand the old and complex texts of the prophet Isaiah from the seat of his royal chariot, total light was suddenly revealed and given to him by the enthusiastic apostle.
premices.p3: Upon his serene descent into the providential water, he became the first formally baptized African high representative as reported in Acts, proudly taking with him the very first living seed of Christianity to transplant it to the deep heart of Africa for its lightning-fast future millennial expansion.
premices.quote1: Now there was an Ethiopian, an eunuch, a high official of Candace, Queen of the Ethiopians, and superintendent of all her treasures. He had come to Jerusalem to worship...

art1.title: St. Augustine of Tagaste
art1.description: Discover the monumental and essential contribution of Northern African theology to the dogma of the Church.

art2.title: Timbuktu Manuscripts
art2.description: A stunning dive into the fierce preservation of sacred scriptures in the heart of Mali's millennial sands.

art3.title: Paleochristian Art
art3.description: How Roman mosaics depicted the vibrant African Christianity of the first centuries.

heritage.title: Heritage and History
heritage.subtitle: Explore the fascinating chronology of 2000 years of continuous Christian presence on the African continent.

event1.title: The Ethiopian Eunuch
event1.desc: The minister of the Candace is baptized by the apostle Philip, planting the very first seeds of the Gospel in the heart of the African continent.

event2.title: The Scillitan Martyrs
event2.desc: First documented traces of flourishing Christianity in North Africa, tragically sealed by the martyrdom of 12 heroic Christians in Carthage.

event3.title: Cyprian of Carthage
event3.desc: Elected brilliant Bishop of Carthage, he unified the African Church during Decius's brutal persecutions and masterfully structured ecclesiological theology.

event4.title: Saint Augustine of Hippo
event4.desc: The greatest thinker of Christian antiquity. His immense works, like The City of God, still shape all of Western theology today.

event5.title: The Council of Chalcedon
event5.desc: Great schism involving the powerful Coptic Church of Egypt which unswervingly maintains its own theological lineage (miaphysitism) throughout the centuries.

event6.title: The Medieval Period
event6.desc: Rapid expansion of Islam in North Africa. The majestic Ethiopian Orthodox Church continues to thrive, isolated in its mountains (rock-hewn churches of Lalibela).

event7.title: First Modern Missions
event7.desc: Arrival of European Catholic missionaries on the coasts (notably in Kongo), leading to courtly Christianity and new complex Christianizations.

event8.title: Renewal and Independence
event8.desc: Strong and vibrant emergence of African Instituted Churches (AICs), reappropriation of faith and incredible demographic explosion of Christianity on the continent.

event9.title: Jubilee of Redemption
event9.desc: Worldwide spiritual celebration of 2000 years of Christian salvation. Africa definitively positions itself as the beating heart and future of universal Christianity.

heritage.contemporarySubtitle: Today's thinkers shaping African Christian thought
heritage.mapDesc: Visually explore the great religious centers from Carthage to Alexandria via Aksum.
heritage.mapPlaceholder: The next step will integrate interactive mapping allowing you to click on Carthage, Hippo, Ethiopia, and Egypt for rich historical details.

riteCongolais.title: The Zairean (Congolese) Rite
riteCongolais.p1: The Roman Missal for the Dioceses of Zaire, promulgated in 1988 by Saint John Paul II, constitutes the most outstanding example of successful liturgical inculturation officially approved by the Holy See.
riteCongolais.p2: Its genius lies in the harmonious integration of the African soul: the rhythmic triumphal entry, invocations of ancestors justified in Christ, and the dialogic form of the homily. The entire body prays, dance becomes liturgy.
